question: Can we push the TileLang selected-limit compressed-dKV producer by grouping D blocks or changing group panel size?

implementation.contract: real final [group,D] fp32 dKV output; same SourceSelected/PairSlots/TileOffsets/ActiveGroups selected-limit route metadata as the retained kernel

tested_candidates.name: grouped_d_small_correctness
tested_candidates.read: The grouped-D kernel is functionally correct on the small gate.

tested_candidates.name: grouped_d_target_d64x2
tested_candidates.failure: requested dynamic shared memory 294912 bytes exceeds MI350 per-block limit 163840 bytes
tested_candidates.read: The useful D64x2 reuse shape is not legal with the full max_active_groups accumulator.

tested_candidates.name: grouped_d_target_d32x2
tested_candidates.read: This legal grouped-D shape regresses because smaller D32 MFMA tiles lose more than P/dP reuse gains.

tested_candidates.name: smaller_group_panel_gp256
tested_candidates.read: Halving the accumulator footprint works but adds panel work and regresses versus GP512.

tested_candidates.name: single_group_panel_gp1024_pc512
tested_candidates.failure: TileLang layout inference failed with no available layout found
tested_candidates.read: The one-panel route is not currently expressible by this TileLang layout.

decision.why: The best legal grouped-D and smaller-panel TileLang variants are slower than the retained 6.18 ms packed / 7.28 ms source-indexed kernel. The useful D64x2 reuse shape exceeds LDS, while the legal D32x2 shape loses MFMA efficiency.
next: Keep the retained selected-limit TileLang dKV body for TorchTitan bridge experiments.
Do not spend more time on full max_active_groups x D grouped-D accumulators in TileLang.

The next real speed branch needs a smaller keyed or segmented in-producer reducer, or a lower-level HIP/CK/rocWMMA implementation that can reduce by compressed group without allocating the full active-group-by-D table.
